components.title: Network Attached Storage (NAS)
components.description: Enterprise network attached storage providing shared file storage over SMB and NFS protocols for file server workloads, backup target storage, and archive storage. Managed via a web administration interface. Storage volumes are encrypted at rest. Active Directory integration provides Windows Authentication for SMB shares.

props.name: protocols-served
props.value: SMB 3.x (primary), NFS v4 (Linux clients), iSCSI (block storage)

props.name: authentication
props.value: Active Directory integration (Windows Authentication for SMB)

protocols.name: smb
protocols.title: SMB — Primary file share access protocol for Windows clients

protocols.name: nfs
protocols.title: NFS — File share access for Linux/Unix clients

protocols.name: iscsi
protocols.title: iSCSI — Block storage access for servers

protocols.name: snmp
protocols.title: SNMP — NAS monitoring (disk health, capacity, RAID status)

protocols.name: syslog
protocols.title: Syslog — Event and audit log forwarding to SIEM

protocols.name: ldap
protocols.title: LDAP — Active Directory integration for Windows Authentication

protocols.name: ldaps
protocols.title: LDAPS — Secure LDAP for Active Directory integration

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Network Attached Storage (NAS).

implemented-requirements.description: An approved hardening configuration is maintained for the NAS, covering: disabling unused protocols (FTP, Telnet, AFP, rsync where not required), disabling the default admin account and creating named administrator accounts, enabling HTTPS-only management (HTTP disabled), enabling SMB signing (SMB 3.x), enabling NFS Kerberos authentication where supported, configuring share-level and filesystem-level access controls (ACLs per share), and keeping NAS firmware current with vendor security updates.

NAS storage volumes containing sensitive or classified data are encrypted at rest using AES-256 volume encryption. Encryption keys are managed securely (not stored in plaintext on the NAS). Encryption status of all volumes is verified and reported in the monthly security review.

Backups of NAS configuration and critical stored data are performed and retained per business continuity requirements. NAS snapshot schedules provide point-in-time recovery. Backup jobs and snapshot jobs are monitored for failure.

Security-relevant NAS events (authentication failures, share access by privileged accounts, configuration changes, failed login attempts, volume mount/unmount events) are centrally logged and forwarded to the SIEM.
